title: OT Access from Untrusted Networks Without Split Tunnel Block

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| iplocation public_ip
| lookup ot_trusted_country.csv Country OUTPUT trust
| where trust!="trusted" OR like(ASN,"*RESIDENTIAL*")
| lookup ot_jump_portals.csv portal OUTPUT sl_t
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, values(public_ip) as ips by user, portal, Country
| sort - last
value: Flags VPN or agent sessions originating from high-risk geos or residential ASNs while accessing OT jump portals.
implementation: (1) Maintain country allowlist aligned to business; (2) require hardware token from untrusted regions; (3) integrate threat intel ASN feed; (4) auto-disable accounts on repeat hits.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks
CIM: Network_Traffic when joined to firewall

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
